title: Node-version preflight: dependency-free first import gates the CLI before node:sqlite can crash it
context: db.ts loads node:sqlite eagerly at module evaluation; on Node < 22.13 (npx/npm run don't enforce engines) every CLI invocation died with a raw ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE stack before Commander could print anything. Hit live: system default Node 20 made `npm test` and the CLI fail cryptically.
decision: src/cli/preflight.ts is a module with ZERO imports, evaluated as the entry point's FIRST import (ESM evaluates a dependency-free first import before the rest of the graph). Too-old Node prints an actionable upgrade message and exits 1 — except `hunch hook`, which emits nothing and exits 0 so an old Node never blocks an agent edit (con_03a0b94b2e).
consequences: clear failure mode for LTS-20 users on every entry path (CLI, mcp, doctor)
the import MUST stay first in src/cli/index.ts — a lint-invisible ordering contract, marked with a comment
hook path degrades to ungrounded-but-unblocked on old Node
alternatives_rejected: Rely on package.json engines: npx/npm run don't enforce it without engine-strict
Lazy-load node:sqlite inside openDb: pushes the crash to first store use with the same raw error, and touches the hot path
Check inside cli/index.ts body: ESM hoists static imports, so the sqlite crash fires before any body code runs
A doctor finding only: doctor itself can't load on old Node
